description: Refactor the Cassini CLI to use `claude -p` subprocess instead of @anthropic-ai/sdk for natural language to SQL translation. This allows the CLI to work with Claude Max/Pro OAuth tokens (via Claude Code) instead of requiring a separate pay-per-use API key. The CLI already works end-to-end — this sprint replaces the AI backend only. The system prompt with Cassini domain context, schema, and sample values is preserved and passed via --system-prompt flag.

tasks.title: Replace Anthropic SDK with claude -p in cli/src/ai.ts
tasks.description:
Rewrite cli/src/ai.ts to use `claude -p` subprocess instead of the Anthropic SDK.

Changes:
- Remove `import Anthropic from '@anthropic-ai/sdk'`
- Keep `buildSystemPrompt(schema, sampleValues)` function as-is — it builds the system prompt with Cassini domain context, schema, and sample column values
- Rewrite `naturalLanguageToSql(question, schema, sampleValues)` to:
  - Strip Claude Code env vars (CLAUDECODE, CLAUDE_CODE_SSE_PORT, CLAUDE_CODE_ENTRYPOINT) from the subprocess env to avoid nested-session errors (same pattern as loop/src/commands/run.ts:598-601)
  - Spawn: `claude -p <question> --system-prompt <systemPrompt> --output-format text --model sonnet --dangerously-skip-permissions --allowedTools ''`
  - Use `--output-format text` (not stream-json) — we just need the final result text
  - Capture stdout as the result text
  - Parse JSON `{ sql, explanation }` from the result (same extraction logic as before — look for JSON object in response)
  - On non-zero exit: capture stderr and throw descriptive error
- Rename `validateApiKey()` to `validateClaude()` — instead of checking ANTHROPIC_API_KEY env var, run `claude --version` and return true if it succeeds, false if claude CLI is not found

Reference implementation: see loop/src/commands/run.ts lines 598-627 for the same `claude -p` pattern with env stripping and stream-json parsing.
acceptanceCriteria: naturalLanguageToSql spawns claude -p subprocess with --system-prompt, --model sonnet, --output-format text
Claude Code env vars are stripped from subprocess environment
Response is parsed as JSON with sql and explanation fields
validateClaude checks for claude CLI availability instead of API key
Errors from the subprocess are caught and rethrown with clear messages

tasks.title: Update commands.ts and repl.ts for new AI interface
tasks.description:
Update the two callsites of the AI module to use the renamed validation function.

Changes to cli/src/commands.ts:
- Change import from `validateApiKey` to `validateClaude`
- Change the call in handleAsk from `validateApiKey()` to `validateClaude()`

Changes to cli/src/repl.ts:
- Change the ANTHROPIC_API_KEY env check warning to instead run validateClaude() — if false, warn that claude CLI is not available but raw SQL still works

The naturalLanguageToSql function signature is unchanged, so no other changes needed in these files.
acceptanceCriteria: commands.ts imports and calls validateClaude instead of validateApiKey
repl.ts warns about missing claude CLI instead of missing ANTHROPIC_API_KEY
handleAsk still validates before calling the AI
REPL still allows raw SQL when claude CLI is unavailable

tasks.title: Remove @anthropic-ai/sdk dependency from cli/package.json
tasks.description:
Remove @anthropic-ai/sdk from cli/package.json dependencies since it is no longer used.

- Remove the @anthropic-ai/sdk entry from the dependencies object
- Run bun install to update the lockfile
- Verify the CLI still builds and runs without the SDK
acceptanceCriteria: bun cli/src/index.ts tables still works (no import errors)
bun cli/src/index.ts ask works with claude -p
